relative_path: Protocols, instructions/Tissue dissociation, organoids and iPDCs/IF for the 384-well and 96-well plate.docx
full_text:
Immunofluorescence for the 384-well plate cultures

Following LIVE-DYE stain

After Live-Dye stain and 40 min fix with 4% paraformaldehyde at RT, cells are left in 75 uL PBS at +4C Färkkilä lab bench fridge. The plate is covered with thin foil, protected from light.

Staining protocol is the same for 384-well and 96-well plate, but the volume of the reagent per well is different. For 96-wells: Add 100uL /well 1xPBS for washing, 100uL /well 1xPBS blocking buffer, 40uL /well antibody mix, finally store the plate with 200uL /well 1xPBS.

Staining

Day 1

Remove the PBS by reverting the plate on top of tissue paper (regular tissue paper from over the sink). Tap the plate a couple of times onto the paper on the bench. Dry the plate with white microscope tissue (kimtech or non-lint).

Add 30 uL per well blocking buffer (3% BSA + 0.3% TritonX-100 in 1x PBS) and incubate at RT for 1 h on the lab bench shielded from light. (Spin down 300g for 5min RT in cell culture centrifuge if needed)

For preparing fresh blocking buffer make 15 mL per 384-well plate (store at +4C up to 1 w if preparing more at once)

Weigh 0.45 g of Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA; in +4C lab fridge top shelf, big container;ID number: PA0005R021, balance on lab bench).

Measure 15 mL of 1x DPBS into a small glass bottle (use serological pipette) and add a stirring magnet inside the bottle. Carefully add BSA into the liquid, close the lid, and place on a magnetic stirrer (inside the Oncosys pre-PCR room) for about 10-30 mins to properly dissolve. Liquid can be left on the stirrer for a longer time also. (clean magnetic stirrer with 1. 70% EtOH, 2. H2O and 3. mQ after use)

Once dissolved, add 45 uL of Triton X-100 (from the lab cupboard). Use unfiltered pipet tips! Since Triton X-100 is very viscous, be careful and patient when pipetting. Once you have taken the proper volume into the pipet tip, discard the tip into the glass bottle to make sure all the volume is added to the BSA-PBS. Leave the solution on a magnetic stirrer for another 15 mins. (you can leave tip and stirrer in bottle for duration of protocol)

Remove blocking buffer from plate and add 15 uL/well antibody cocktail prepared in blocking buffer. Spin down 300g 5min at RT. Incubate O/N at +4C cold room shaker sealed with parafilm and tin foil. (incubate at least 12h)

Day 2

Next day, add 50 uL of 1x PBS (from the tank) per well and remove the liquid.

Wash the wells 3 times with 50-70 uL 1x PBS to remove any Triton x-100 residues. (add washes if necessary)

Note! Triton causes bubbles. Wash the wells with 70 uL of 1x PBS if possible, but reduce the volume if there are disturbing bubbles in the wells.

Use cell culture centrifuge to remove bubbles: change the rotor for the plate holder and spin at 300 g for 5 min, RT. (always make sure the rotor is tightened properly before use!)

After washing, leave 50 uL 1x PBS per well. Seal the plate lid with parafilm, and cover the plate with tin foil. Mark the tin foil with Sample ID, staining, date and initials (e.g. S150_pOme; live-dead / CK7 / CD45 / Hoechst; 1.8.2022 eap). Place the plate inside +4C fridge.

Image with OperaPhenix scanner

Antibody cocktail:

Prepare for 5 mL for full 384 well plate ((14 x 22 x 15) uL x 1.1)

(from 384 wells only 308 in use, small amount of excess prepared, cocktail prepared in 1x non-reusable reservoir or max 1h before needed in blocking buffer in 15ml falcon and kept at +4C protected from light)

CK7- 555; in -20C box 2; small aliquots; #ab209601

Dilution 1:250 🡪 20 uL

CD45-647; in +4C lab fridge, Box 1; cat: 304018

Dilution 1:150 🡪 33.3 uL

Hoechst 33342; in +4C lab fridge door; original tube

Dilution 1:8000 🡪 0.63 uL (0.8 ug/mL final concentration)

Notes

Washing steps: use non-sterile RT 1x PBS from sink tank

Blocking buffer: use RT 1x DPBS for cell culture for preparing block solution

non reusable reservoirs for pipetting antibody cocktail, reusable reservoir for washing
